text.title: PM 1:1 Template for Engineering and Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every PM 1:1 Template for Engineering and Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Separate template per partner role: EM (capacity, blockers, hiring); design lead (research, system, capacity); support (top issues, escalations). One-size template fails.
rule.rationale: Role-specific concerns belong in the template; generic templates produce empty meetings.

rule.statement: Every 1:1 ends with ≥1 action item, owner, deadline. Logged in shared tracker. Untracked items = no follow-through.
rule.rationale: Without tracker, 1:1s become venting; with tracker, they compound.

rule.statement: Both parties add agenda items async ≥4h before. No prep = reschedule.
rule.rationale: Walking-in-cold 1:1s default to gossip; async prep enforces seriousness.

rule.statement: If both parties have nothing prepared, cancel (do not 'show up anyway'). 1:1 calendar is sacred, not the meeting itself.
rule.rationale: Forced 1:1s create resentment; explicit skip rule respects calendar.

rule.statement: Quarterly: rate the 1:1 (signal/noise, action-item completion). Below threshold = restructure or change frequency.
rule.rationale: 1:1s decay silently; explicit review prevents zombie meetings.
